briefs.title: Council to consider Land Acknowledgement for Langley City meeting
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will open its April 23, 2026 meeting with a Land Acknowledgement recognizing the traditional unceded territory of the Katzie, Kwantlen, Matsqui, and Semiahmoo First Nations. This is a ceremonial statement and does not propose any new rules, bylaws, or requirements for businesses.

briefs.title: Council to consider allocating $168,000 in 2026 Community Grants for Langley organizations
briefs.summary: Langley City Council will consider how to distribute up to $168,000 in community grants for 2026, funded by casino proceeds. Applications totalling over $344,000 have been received, more than double the available budget, so Council must decide which organizations get funded and how much each receives.
briefs.action_required: If your organization applied for a 2026 Community Grant, watch for the Council decision on allocations.

briefs.title: Council to consider: Langley City Committee of the Whole meeting — April 2026
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on entering a Committee of the Whole session at its April 23, 2026 meeting. A Committee of the Whole is a format where all council members meet together to discuss and debate specific agenda items more openly before making formal decisions.
briefs.action_required: Check the full Langley City Council agenda for April 23, 2026 to see which items will be debated.

key_dates.label: Langley City Committee of the Whole meeting takes place

briefs.title: Council to consider: Committee of the Whole rise and report — Langley City
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on a procedural motion to have the Committee of the Whole — a meeting format where all councillors discuss items together — rise (formally end) and report its findings back to the full Council. This is a standard housekeeping step in the meeting process and does not introduce new rules or requirements for businesses.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Ratifying Committee of the Whole Recommendations — Langley City
briefs.summary: Langley City Council is set to vote on ratifying — meaning formally approving — recommendations that came out of a recent Committee of the Whole meeting. The agenda does not specify which recommendations are included, so business owners should check the full meeting package for details that may affect them.
briefs.action_required: Review the full Langley City Council meeting package to see which recommendations are up for ratification.
